videos.summary_source_text:
Texas Rangers Facing 10-Game Road Trip At Key Part Of Season | DLLS Rangers Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

An ALLCITY Network Production

The Rangers are on the road for the next 10 games, no days off, ample pitfalls ahead; Rangers are 18-22 on the road this season.
This trip is important because the trade deadline is nearing. When it’s over July 1, the Rangers will have nine games, all at home, before the All-Star break and then two weeks until the trade deadline.
One of the two key cogs of the Rangers’ offense has found his footing. Langford came off the IL on June 5. He is batting .316 (19-60) with five home runs.
What can we expect? We have to hope he stays healthy; he’s already missed more than a month, so keeping him on the field is paramount; could he match last season’s 20-20 campaign? Yes. Could improve upon it? He hit only .241 last season; he’s at .271 now; needs more walks

videos.title: Lär dig förstå den magiska cirkeln, del 1/3. Uppvärmning.
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Lär dig förstå den magiska cirkeln, del 1/3. Uppvärmning. Att använda uppvärmningen till att förstå rätt sving och armrörelse är en genväg till bättre golf. #golf #golfswing #golftips 

0:00 Intro till lär dig den magiska cirkeln från grunden. 

Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. Att använda uppvärmningen till att förstå rätt sving och armrörelse är en genväg till bättre golf. #golf #golfswing #golftips

videos.title: 22.06.26 - RIFT Education Day
videos.summary_source_text:
22.06.26 - RIFT Education Day 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

Agent Operating System Q&A: Updates, Hermes Migration, Hardware, Fusion vs Fugu + Jarvis Voice Control

The episode answers recent AI Profit Boardroom questions about using and updating a linked “agent operating system” that includes agents for news, memory (“memory galaxy”), music, video, SEO, and more, with daily updates delivered via zip files and an update command/markdown. It highlights community contributions like generating local AI avatar videos on an RTX 5090 and adding memory to agents. The host explains migrating a Claude-based agent OS to Hermes by having Claude orchestrate and integrate Hermes, recommends a MacBook Pro for frequent travel when not running local models, and warns about the “illusion of progress,” advising time audits and eliminating/delegating before automating. It covers token-limit management, free NVIDIA API use in Hermes, replacing Gemini CLI with Antigravity, using Claude without an API key via CLI, integrating OpenRouter Fusion and Sakana Fugu (with benchmarks and long generation times), and demonstrates a Jarvis-style real-time, voice-activated Hermes setup plus Obsidian customization and Boardroom training/coaching resources.

videos.title: Hindi - 22.06.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi - 22.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

videos.title: Will We See More Star Players On The Move? Is This a New Trend in the NHL?
videos.summary_source_text:
Will We See More Star Players On The Move? Is This a New Trend in the NHL? Florida Panthers shake up the NHL with the blockbuster Brady Tkachuk trade — are more franchise stars like Connor McDavid and Auston Matthews next on the move? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson examine the rising trend of marquee player trades, the power of no-move clauses, and how teams like the Ottawa Senators and Detroit Red Wings might respond after losing key pieces.

The conversation spotlights the ripple effect on Stanley Cup odds, with Colorado Avalanche holding the top spot and teams like Dallas Stars and Winnipeg Jets facing uncertainty. Key debates include Jason Robertson’s future, Connor Hellebuyck’s potential exit, and Quinn Hughes’ extension negotiations. Plus, Chris and Zach award Sam Malinsky a season grade, exploring his growth on the Avalanche blue line. Are NHL superstars set to shape a new era of player-driven team building — and which contender is most likely to break through?

Eric Bolte-Imagn Images

videos.title: Knicks Win It All, Giannis Trade Rumors & World Cup Fever | Mood Swings with Liv Moods
videos.summary_source_text:
Knicks Win It All, Giannis Trade Rumors & World Cup Fever | Mood Swings with Liv Moods The New York Knicks are NBA champions, Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors are heating up, and the FIFA World Cup has completely taken over the sports world.

On this episode of Mood Swings presented by Landmark Lincoln, Liv Moods breaks down the Knicks' impressive championship run led by Jalen Brunson, why Giannis may finally need a fresh start away from Milwaukee, and whether the Boston Celtics or Miami Heat make more sense as his next destination. She also dives into the Denver Nuggets' biggest offseason questions, including potential roster changes and what Denver needs to do to get back into championship contention around Nikola Jokić.

Plus, Liv shares her biggest World Cup takeaways, including Team USA's surprising rise, Lionel Messi continuing to prove why he's the GOAT, Cristiano Ronaldo's struggles, and why this year's tournament has become must-watch television.

🏀 Knicks championship reaction
🏀 Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors
🏀 Boston Celtics vs. Miami Heat landing spots
🏀 Denver Nuggets offseason discussion
🏀 Nikola Jokić and roster moves
⚽ FIFA World Cup reactions
⚽ Team USA World Cup performance
⚽ Lionel Messi vs. Cristiano Ronaldo

videos.title: Talking Trades & NHL Draft | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Talking Trades & NHL Draft | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3 of Morning Cuppa Hockey continues as Jonny Lazarus and former NHL defenceman Colby Cohen bring you another loaded episode with the 2026 NHL Draft just days away in Buffalo. James van Riemsdyk joins the show fresh off a season in Detroit and officially hitting the open market as an unrestricted free agent — and with 17 years in the league across Philly, Toronto, Boston, Columbus, and the Red Wings, JVR has a perspective on player movement, life in Canadian markets, and the growing trend of American players gravitating south of the border that nobody else can quite match.

Then the guys shift to the draft floor as two of the most intriguing prospects in this class stop by. 

Tynan Lawrence — the two-way centre from Fredericton who won the Clark Cup and playoff MVP with Muskegon before making his mid-season jump to Boston University — breaks down what this week feels like with his name expected to be called in the top ten on Friday night in Buffalo. 

And Keaton Verhoeff, the 6'4 right-shot defenceman from North Dakota who's drawn comparisons to Drew Doughty and sits among the most coveted prospects in the entire draft class, talks about what it was like to hold his own in the NCAA as a 17-year-old, the questions about his skating, and what comes next once his name gets called.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

🎙️ Subscribe to the Morning Cuppa Hockey podcast — Apple, Spotify, wherever you listen.

That started the domino effect that is team USA hockey players wanting to move to contenders in the NHL. Now, Brady Tkachuk is heading to the Florida Panthers to play with his brother and team USA teammate Matthew Tkachuk, who has won two Stanley Cups in the last three years.

Is Team USAs dynamic going to change the scope of the league, and will we see more big names request trades in the coming weeks??With more trade news heating up as the draft approaches, tune into our league-wide NHL show Wednesdays at 1:00 MST on X, Facebook, and YouTube👀

videos.title: Who Should the Nuggets’ TOP Targets be in the NBA Draft? | w/ Draft Expert Will Gottlieb
videos.summary_source_text:
Who Should the Nuggets’ TOP Targets be in the NBA Draft? | w/ Draft Expert Will Gottlieb The 2026 NBA Draft is here, and the Denver Nuggets hold the No. 26 pick with major questions about whether they should stay put, trade up, or move back. Will Gottlieb joins Harrison Wind of the DNVR Nuggets Podcast to break down Denver’s top NBA Draft targets, including Zuby Ejiofor, Joshua Jefferson, Koa Peat, Bennett Stirtz, and other potential fits around Nikola Jokić, Jamal Murray, and the Nuggets’ championship core. From defensive versatility and rim protection to shooting, basketball IQ, rookie-scale contracts, and second-apron roster building, we dive into what the Nuggets need most on draft night. Who should Denver target in the first round, and which prospect would be the perfect fit for the Nuggets?

videos.title: Why Do The Best Players Bend Their Ankles So Much?
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Do The Best Players Bend Their Ankles So Much? The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Are The Ottawa Senators REALLY Thinking About Offer-Sheeting Jason Robertson? | DLLS Stars Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Are The Ottawa Senators REALLY Thinking About Offer-Sheeting Jason Robertson? | DLLS Stars Podcast Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

There was a blockbuster trade in the NHL on Sunday, as the Ottawa Senators traded captain Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers, and older brother Matthew Tkachuk, in exchange for three first round draft picks (two in 2026, one [conditional] in 2029) and a 2027 second round pick. Rumors then started swirling that with a gaping hole on their top line, and lots of newly acquired draft capital, that Ottawa is planning on making a hard push to trade for Dallas Stars pending restricted free agent Jason Robertson. And if that effort is unsuccessful, then the Senators might attempt to sign Robertson to a massive offer-sheet contract. So, let’s dive into the latest news and talk, once more, about Jason Robertson. Plus, a whole bunch of other NHL signings and trades happened over the weekend, involving Darren Raddysh, Jason Dickinson, Bobby McMann, Mackie Samoskevich and Nicolas Deslauriers, which happened during the Carolina Hurricanes Stanley Cup championship parade.

videos.title: Understand and learn the circle of speed. Part 1 of 3. Warm up.
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Understand and learn the circle of speed. Part 1 of 3. Warm up. Everything we do affects how we think about the golf swing. Use the circle of speed in all parts of you practice, even your warm up. #golfswing #golf #golftips 


0:00 Intro to when to bend your lead arm/elbow

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly.

videos.title: More Trades are Coming! Hellebuyck, Pettersson, DeBrusk & More Rumors Heating Up w/ Dave Pagnotta
videos.summary_source_text:
More Trades are Coming! Hellebuyck, Pettersson, DeBrusk & More Rumors Heating Up w/ Dave Pagnotta The NHL trade market is heating up.

David Pagnotta joins Tyler Yaremchuk and Carter Hutton to break down the latest trade rumors from around the league, including growing speculation surrounding Connor Hellebuyck, Elias Pettersson, Brock Boeser, Jake DeBrusk, Dylan Larkin, and several other notable NHL names.

Pagnotta explains why the Winnipeg Jets' situation with Hellebuyck is drawing more attention, what the Vancouver Canucks could be looking to do with Pettersson and Boeser, and why multiple teams are expected to be aggressive ahead of NHL Draft weekend.

The conversation also touches on Ottawa's plans following the Brady Tkachuk blockbuster, the latest on Jason Robertson and Mason McTavish, Florida's search for goaltending, Seattle's desire to make a splash this summer, and why Pagnotta believes more major trades could be coming soon.

If you're looking for the latest NHL trade rumors, insider information, and offseason buzz, this is a must-watch.

videos.title: THE 99¢ BLOOD PRESSURE DRINK | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
THE 99¢ BLOOD PRESSURE DRINK | Dr. Mandell Most people think high blood pressure starts in the heart, but in many cases it begins in the blood vessels long before symptoms appear. In this video, Dr. Mandell explains why healthy arteries are so important for circulation, how nitric oxide helps blood vessels relax, and why one inexpensive drink found in most grocery stores has attracted attention from researchers around the world. Learn how hibiscus tea may support vascular health through its powerful antioxidants and plant compounds. Discover the simple way to prepare it by steeping one hibiscus tea bag or one to two teaspoons of dried hibiscus flowers in hot water for 5 to 10 minutes. Enjoy it hot or chilled over ice, and add a squeeze of lemon if desired. You'll also learn additional lifestyle habits that support healthy blood pressure, including proper nutrition, potassium-rich foods, magnesium, walking, deep breathing, stress reduction, and hydration. Sometimes the most powerful health habits are also the simplest and most affordable. Watch until the end and learn why this bright red tea may be one of the most overlooked drinks for supporting healthy blood vessels, circulation, and long-term cardiovascular wellness.

videos.title: Brady Tkachuk Traded to Florida + Brandon Bussi Joins the Show ft. David Pagnotta | The Sheet
videos.summary_source_text:
Brady Tkachuk Traded to Florida + Brandon Bussi Joins the Show ft. David Pagnotta | The Sheet The NHL offseason took a dramatic turn on today's episode of The Sheet as Jeff Marek is joined by David Pagnotta to break down the blockbuster trade that sent Brady Tkachuk from the Ottawa Senators to the Florida Panthers, reuniting him with his brother Matthew Tkachuk. Jeff and David dive into what led to Brady's decision to seek a move, the package heading back to Ottawa, what this means for both franchises moving forward, and the growing trend of American stars departing Canadian markets. They also discuss the latest coaching extensions, contract signings, offseason rumors, and the biggest stories shaping the NHL landscape as teams continue to prepare for the 2026-27 season. Later, Stanley Cup champion Brandon Bussi joins the show to reflect on the Carolina Hurricanes' championship run, the celebration that followed, his journey to becoming an NHL goaltender, what he learned throughout the season, and what's next for both himself and the Hurricanes after reaching hockey's ultimate goal.

📺 Subscribe for more NHL news, analysis, interviews, trade breakdowns, and offseason coverage from The Sheet with Jeff Marek.

videos.title: Ultimate Guide To ChatGPT Codex for Everyday People
videos.summary_source_text:
Ultimate Guide To ChatGPT Codex for Everyday People *Free Codex Prompt Pack:* https://clickhubspot.com/7gyw

I walk through how I use ChatGPT Codex to get real work done on my computer.
Download ChatGPT Codex for Mac or PC here: https://chatgpt.com/codex/

I show how Codex works, how to set it up, and how to use it even if you do not know how to code. Codex can help with files, folders, spreadsheets, PDFs, receipts, images, websites, Gmail, Google Drive, Chrome, Canva, and more.

I use Codex to turn receipt images into an Excel dashboard. I also show how it can read PDF invoices and make a clean spreadsheet. Then I use Codex to clean up a messy folder, rename files, and sort them into the right places.

I also walk through Codex plugins, skills, memory, automations, computer use, and website building. I show where Codex is useful, where it still feels slow, and how I think about giving it access to files and apps.

This is a simple beginner guide to ChatGPT Codex. I made it to help you see what Codex can do beyond coding, and how you can use it for everyday work. *Free Codex Prompt Pack:* https://clickhubspot.com/7gyw

videos.title: The Backswing Move That Felt Wrong But Instantly Improved His Ball Striking
videos.summary_source_text:
The Backswing Move That Felt Wrong But Instantly Improved His Ball Striking ⛳️ Take Your FREE Game Assessment → https://meandmygolf.com/game-plan/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=22.06.26

📱 Download the Swing Coach App (2-Week Free Trial) → https://go.meandmygolf.com/swingcoach/yt/?utm_source=YouTube&utm_medium=Description&utm_campaign=22.06.26

Struggling with your iron ball striking and direction? In this lesson, we worked with Ian to uncover the real reason his contact was so inconsistent.

He wasn’t just fighting his hands or club-face. The bigger issue was in his backswing. His lower body was swaying, his posture was changing, and his head and spine were moving forward, which led to poor contact, shanks, and inconsistent direction.
Once we improved his pivot and helped him maintain his posture, everything changed. His strike improved, his accuracy got better, and the shank disappeared.
If your irons feel inconsistent, this is a great lesson in why the right movement matters more than just trying harder.

videos.title: PARADE OF CHAMPIONS! 180K Fans Flock to Downtown Raleigh for Carolina Hurricanes Stanley Cup Parade
videos.summary_source_text:
PARADE OF CHAMPIONS! 180K Fans Flock to Downtown Raleigh for Carolina Hurricanes Stanley Cup Parade The 2026 Stanley Cup Parade in downtown Raleigh was a movie on Saturday morning/afternoon. Over 180,000 Carolina Hurricanes fans flocked to downtown to celebrate their champions. There were projections of 100K fans to show up; however, the Caniacs came out in masses for the Hurricanes. In an area that has college rivalries, the Hurricanes bring everyone together. On the Monday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Stanley Cup parade as a whole. The crowds, the noise, the overall celebration were something you had to be there in person to understand the magnitude of the event. It was single-handedly the biggest crowd for an event in the history of Raleigh. Caniacs officially put the hockey world on notice that Raleigh is a hockey city, point blank, end of story. That said, what were the overall vibes there? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the Nic Deslauriers extension that was announced during the rally portion of the festivities. Apparently, only Rod Brind'Amour, Eric Tulsky, some of the Hurricanes staff, and Deslauriers knew about the deal. No one else had a clue. When have you ever seen an extension get announced at a Stanley Cup parade? Finally, Zach concludes the show by talking about what's next for the Hurricanes. Now that the parade is over and the celebrations continue, what's next on the agenda before the official start to the next NHL season on July 1? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: The BIGGEST takeaway of the Denver Broncos’ offseason after OTAs and minicamp
videos.summary_source_text:
The BIGGEST takeaway of the Denver Broncos’ offseason after OTAs and minicamp The Denver Broncos’ 2026 offseason program is officially complete. What was the biggest lesson we learned from Sean Payton’s OTAs and mandatory minicamp?
The DNVR Broncos crew breaks down Bo Nix’s recovery and progression entering Year 3, Jaylen Waddle’s early impact on Sean Payton’s offense and the players who strengthened their cases before training camp. Which position battles are beginning to take shape? Which young Broncos generated the most buzz? And did Denver show enough this spring to raise expectations for the 2026 NFL season?
Plus, the guys discuss Sean Payton, Davis Webb, Courtland Sutton, Pat Bryant, Marvin Mims Jr., J.K. Dobbins, the Broncos’ defense and everything else learned during Denver’s offseason practices.

On the DNVR Broncos Podcast, Super Bowl 50 champion Bennie Fowler, Zac Stevens, Ryan Koenigsberg and Henry Chisholm are coming to you LIVE from the DNVR Bar to break down all of the latest news in Broncos Country.

videos.title: Cowork + DeepSeek: Microsoft Just Stopped Caring Which AI Model Wins
videos.summary_source_text:
Cowork + DeepSeek: Microsoft Just Stopped Caring Which AI Model Wins Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Microsoft didn't announce a new model this week — it announced something bigger. Copilot Cowork went generally available worldwide on June 16, 2026, and buried in the docs is a shift that matters more than any single model: Microsoft is rebuilding Copilot into a routed, multi-model fabric where a cheap Chinese model like DeepSeek V4 could quietly run parts of your enterprise AI agents.

In this video I separate the CONFIRMED facts from the REPORTED headlines (there's a big gap), break down what Cowork actually is, follow the money behind the model-picker strategy, and unpack the geopolitics of putting DeepSeek inside a US enterprise product.

What we cover:
- What Microsoft officially confirmed vs. what Axios only reported
- Copilot Cowork as an agentic, long-running enterprise operating layer
- The model picker — Auto, Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.5 — and the door DeepSeek walks through
- The real reason behind a "cheaper lane": usage-based billing on model use, context, tool calls, and runtime
- The price gap (DeepSeek V4 Flash vs GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.8 on Azure)
- The tool-calling caveat enterprise teams can't wave away
- Why the orchestration layer — not the model — is becoming the real moat

The thesis: enterprise AI agents are becoming multi-model, usage-metered, and cloud-governed by default — with or without DeepSeek getting confirmed.

🔔 Subscribe to BitBiased AI for skepticism-first AI coverage that separates verified facts from vendor hype.

#microsoftcopilot  #deepseek  #enterpriseai

00:59 Confirmed vs Reported
1:43 What Cowork Actually Is

6:49 The Bigger War
7:30 The Thesis

- Axios report on Cowork + DeepSeek exploration
- Reuters reporting on US government DeepSeek concerns

videos.title: Senators Trade Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Senators Trade Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers | Daily Faceoff Live The NHL offseason just delivered its biggest blockbuster.

Tyler Yaremchuk and Carter Hutton react to the stunning trade that sends Brady Tkachuk from the Ottawa Senators to the Florida Panthers, reuniting him with brother Matthew Tkachuk and creating one of the most intimidating lineups in hockey.

Are the Panthers now the team to beat in the Eastern Conference? How does Brady fit into Florida's championship window? And what does this mean for the rest of the NHL?

The conversation then shifts to Ottawa. Reports suggest the Senators are looking to flip some of the assets acquired in the deal and continue pushing to contend next season. What players should Steve Staios target? Could another major move be around the corner?

Plus, the guys discuss whether the Minnesota Wild or Vegas Golden Knights could make a serious push for Dylan Larkin, and whether the growing trend of star players wanting to play with friends is ultimately good for the game.

David Pagnotta joins for The Insider Notebook to discuss Ottawa's next steps, the Jason Dickinson extension in Edmonton, Jake Walman's future, Seattle's offseason plans, and what type of addition the Montreal Canadiens are hoping to make this summer.

videos.title: 314 Billion Parameters vs Microsoft: Inside Musk's $2B "Macrohard"
videos.summary_source_text:
314 Billion Parameters vs Microsoft: Inside Musk's $2B "Macrohard" Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk says he can simulate an entire software company with AI — no employees, no offices, just Grok and a Tesla agent clicking through real software on its own. It's called Macrohard, and after digging through the trademark filings, the Reuters reporting, and the leaked internal mess, the truth is messier than the headline. The trademark is real. The $2 billion is real. But the version Musk is selling and the version that actually exists are two very different things.

In this breakdown I separate what's been CONFIRMED from what's still just a tweet — how the brain-and-hand architecture actually works, why the 600-contractor training program got paused, how it stacks up against GPT-4, Claude, Gemini and Llama, and why betting on Musk's vision is reasonable but betting on his timeline keeps burning people.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 The Joke That's Actually Real
01:15 Background: Why This Isn't Coming Out of Nowhere
02:20 What Is Macrohard, Actually?
03:34 The Timeline: How We Got Here
05:06 How It Actually Works
07:12 How It Stacks Up Against Everyone Else
08:36 What It Could Actually Do
09:51 The Risks Nobody Should Skip
11:31 The Business Model: Who Actually Pays?
12:38 The Musk Track Record

Do you think Macrohard reshapes how software gets built — or is it another idea that's five years from being two years away? Drop it in the comments. If this helped you separate signal from Musk-noise, hit subscribe.

videos.title: I’ve Never Seen Ball Striking Improve This Quickly
videos.summary_source_text:
I’ve Never Seen Ball Striking Improve This Quickly Ball striking is one of the biggest frustrations in golf, but what if one simple feel could change your contact almost instantly?

In this video, I share the exact move I have been using with my students to create cleaner strikes, better compression, and more consistent iron shots. The results have been incredible, and the best part is that it doesn’t require a complicated swing rebuild.

Most golfers spend years chasing perfect positions, when often the biggest improvements come from understanding the correct feel through impact.

videos.title: Difficult decisions for Ferrari + Mercedes? | Austrian GP Preview | F1 Nation Podcast
videos.summary_source_text:
Difficult decisions for Ferrari + Mercedes? | Austrian GP Preview | F1 Nation Podcast Tom Clarkson is joined by former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer and IndyCar race winner James Hinchcliffe to preview this weekend’s Austrian Grand Prix.

With Lewis Hamilton’s victory in Barcelona putting him just 41 points behind championship leader Kimi Antonelli, do Ferrari and Mercedes have some tough decisions to make?

If Charles Leclerc doesn’t bounce back in the next few races, could Ferrari ask him to play a supporting role in a Hamilton title challenge? How would Charles respond to that?

And with the threat of Ferrari lurking, will Mercedes change their approach to how they let Antonelli and George Russell race each other?

Plus, the guys discuss what challenge the Red Bull Ring provides and share their podium predictions. Will Red Bull be strong at their home track? Are McLaren getting closer to a Grand Prix victory? And why might reliability be a particularly hot topic in Spielberg?

For more F1® videos, visit: https://www.Formula1.com

videos.title: Tkachuks Re-United | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Tkachuks Re-United | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3 of Morning Cuppa Hockey is bringing you a full breakdown of the biggest trade of the NHL offseason — Brady Tkachuk is headed to the Florida Panthers, and Jonny Lazarus and Colby Cohen are here to unpack every angle of a deal that just reshuffled the entire Eastern Conference.

Brady Tkachuk is joining his brother Matthew in South Florida after the Ottawa Senators shipped their captain to the Panthers in exchange for three first-round picks and a second — including the 9th and 25th overall selections in the 2026 NHL Draft. It's a blockbuster that raises enormous questions on both sides: What does it mean for Ottawa's rebuild? And just how dangerous is this Panthers roster now?

With two years remaining on his deal at a cap hit of $8.2 million, Brady gives Florida a two-way weapon who was a menace defensively even through an injury-shortened season — and the prospect of him lining up alongside Matthew and Sam Bennett is something opposing defences will have nightmares about. Florida missed the playoffs this season after a rash of injuries — most notably Aleksander Barkov missing the entire year — but with this move they're already being talked about as front-runners out of the East once again. 

Jonny and Colby dig into what the Sens get back, whether Steve Staios pulled the trigger at the right time, and what the Tkachuk reunion means for a Panthers team that's won two of the last three Stanley Cups. Then to close things out, The Fourth Period's David Pagnotta stops by with the latest from around the league as the NHL rumour mill kicks into overdrive heading into draft week.

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

🎙️ Subscribe to the Morning Cuppa Hockey podcast — Apple, Spotify, wherever you listen.

Sakana Fugu Ultra vs OpenRouter Fusion: Side-by-Side Tests (Beating Fable 5?)

The video compares two new “panel” APIs—Sakana’s Fugu (Ultra and Mini) and OpenRouter Fusion—both claiming benchmark performance above Fable 5, by testing them side by side on visual/code generations like a landing page, raycaster maze, living spiral galaxy, inner solar system, and other simulations. In these examples, Fugu Ultra consistently produces the best-looking, smoothest outputs, Fusion is generally second, and Fugu Mini is faster but more variable and sometimes buggy. The script notes practical tradeoffs: Sakana/Fugu is cheaper with a flat plan but is heavily limited by daily generation/time caps and token limits, while Fusion is easier to use via API but costs per token. Both are one-prompt systems with long waits, and both are integrated into the creator’s Agent operating system and AI Profit Boardroom community.

Dessutom är det bra att planera flera år i förväg för att ge sig själv de bästa förutsättningarna. Ändå är det här vårt första hela avsnitt om hur du faktiskt tar ut pensionen.

Jag tar hjälp av Monica Sjödin, en av Sveriges bästa pensionsexperter, för att reda ut det som för många är de svåraste pensionsbesluten. Monicas stora poäng är: uttagsordningen är fel fråga. Rätt uttag avgörs av tre saker:

▸ Ditt kapitalbehov - när och hur mycket pengar du behöver
▸ Din skattesituation - hur uttagen av din pension kommer beskattas
▸ Din risktolerans - hur mycket risk du kan och är villig att acceptera

Vi pratar om varför det förhöjda grundavdraget gör tidiga uttag dyra, lunchrumsrådet att ta ut hela pensionen direkt (jättedåligt för de flesta) och jobba heltid som kan kosta ungefär tio plus tio procentenheter i skatt, 2-minutersövningen på minPension.se som visar ditt pensionsgap och varför du bör räkna med pensionen även om du siktar på att gå tidigt.

⏱️ Kapitel:

00:04:10 - Vad pensionsuttag är: kapitalbehov, skatt och risk

00:37:00 - Tänk risk i riskklasser i stället för aktier och räntor
00:42:50 - För rädd att ta ut pension tidigt: vad det kostar
00:45:25 - Den rika fattigpensionären och att belåna boendet
00:50:25 - Ju mindre pengar du har desto viktigare med smarta beslut
00:52:10 - Pension och boende utomlands som Portugal
00:54:15 - Tidig pension och varför unga bör spara buffert
00:59:05 - Den emotionella sidan: pensionera dig till något
01:00:55 - Monicas kurser och vad de innehåller
01:03:55 - Är det någonsin kört för pensionen?

Jag tar hjälp av Monica Sjödin, en av Sveriges bästa pensionsexperter, för att reda ut det som för många är de svåraste pensionsbesluten. Monicas stora poäng är: uttagsordningen är fel fråga. Rätt uttag avgörs av tre saker:

▸ Ditt kapitalbehov - när och hur mycket pengar du behöver
▸ Din skattesituation - hur uttagen av din pension kommer beskattas
▸ Din risktolerans - hur mycket risk du kan och är villig att acceptera

Vi pratar om varför det förhöjda grundavdraget gör tidiga uttag dyra, lunchrumsrådet att ta ut hela pensionen direkt (jättedåligt för de flesta) och jobba heltid som kan kosta ungefär tio plus tio procentenheter i skatt, 2-minutersövningen på minPension.se som visar ditt pensionsgap och varför du bör räkna med pensionen även om du siktar på att gå tidigt.

videos.title: This is why you HAVE to get fitted for wedges 👀
videos.summary_source_text:
This is why you HAVE to get fitted for wedges 👀 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

videos.title: What I Learned From a Tour Pro - The Cold Truth
videos.summary_source_text:
What I Learned From a Tour Pro - The Cold Truth How far off is a scratch golfer or content creator from a current DP World Tour professional? Today, I’m exposing every single weakness in my golf game by going head-to-head with a Tour Pro in a brutal 9-hole match play test to see exactly where I lose strokes.

Using the brand new updates in the Arccos app, we are looking at our performance "shots gained per shot" to get an exact data overview of how my game stacks up to elite tour standards. From wayward drives off the tee to crucial putting stats, this was a massive wake-up call for my ego, but an incredible lesson in what it actually takes to play professional golf. If you want to know how to improve your golf game and understand the real difference between a regular golfer and a pro, the stats at the end of this video tell the whole story.

What You Will Learn in This Video:

1. The exact strokes gained differences between an amateur/creator and a Tour Pro.
2. Why avoiding penalty shots is the fastest way to drop your handicap.
3. How professional golfers manage their short game and putting under pressure to avoid mistakes.
4. How to use data tracking to pinpoint your personal golf weaknesses.

0:00 - How Good is a Tour Pro? (The Ultimate Wake-Up Call)
0:46 - Hole 1: The Slaying Begins (Rough Start)
2:10 - Hole 2: Professional Eye-Balling vs. Clowns
3:18 - Hole 3: Mind Games & Miraculous Pars
4:44 - Hole 4: Overpowering the Course vs. Old Man Strategy
6:25 - Hole 5: The Wheels Start Coming Off
7:37 - Hole 6: The Great Cart Path Disaster
9:15 - Hole 7: Pointless Tapping & Finishing Up
10:28 - The Cold Truth: Strokes Gained Data Breakdown

videos.title: Always Tired? You're Missing THIS (NOT Magnesium)
videos.summary_source_text:
Always Tired? You're Missing THIS (NOT Magnesium) Your fatigue, poor sleep, and muscle weakness may not be from aging… it could be from one of the most overlooked nutrient deficiencies in the world.

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4a7HvDx

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

Most people focus on magnesium, vitamin D, or zinc, but potassium is the nutrient your body depends on every second of the day. It powers your nerves, muscles, brain, energy levels, and even helps regulate blood pressure through something called the sodium-potassium pump.

The shocking part? Most people don’t even come close to the recommended 4700 mg per day.

If you’ve been struggling with low energy, muscle fatigue, poor recovery, or restless sleep, this video could completely change how you look at nutrition.

Dr. Eric Berg, DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the author of the best-selling book "The Healthy Keto Plan" and is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals. He no longer practices but focuses on health education through social media.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. If you struggle with consistency in your golf swing, this golf lesson gives you one simple thing to prioritize: a stable axis. Eric Cogorno shows the exact golf drills he gives students to keep the spine centered and the body's inclination to the ground constant during the backswing and downswing. These golf tips work whether you're working on your golf swing from face on or down the line, and they apply to any golfer who wants to learn how to play golf with more consistent ball striking. If you leave this video without trying the airplane drill, you're leaving consistency on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• What a stable axis is and why it's the key to a consistent golf swing
• How to use the Swing Coach App to measure spine movement during your swing
• Why high shoulder turn and a dropping chest causes excess head movement
• The airplane drill for keeping your head centered from face on
• How trail side extension and lead side tilt work together during the backswing and downswing
• Why keeping your body's inclination to the ground constant improves ball striking
• The hands-on-legs drill for training left side tilt from down the line
• How to combine both drills for a simple, repeatable, consistent golf swing

Want to know why you're slicing, hooking, hitting fat shots, or struggling with consistency? In this video, a golf instructor with over 30 years of teaching experience and more than 40,000 lessons breaks down the three key factors that determine every golf shot: club path, clubface position, and setup. Understanding these fundamentals is the fastest way to identify what's really causing your swing problems.

You'll learn how to diagnose an over-the-top swing, understand why inside-out swings create hooks, and discover how clubface control influences ball flight. The video also explains the relationship between path and face, why many golfers misdiagnose their swing faults, and how small changes can dramatically improve ball striking, accuracy, and distance.

Whether you're a beginner golfer or a seasoned player looking to lower your scores, this lesson provides a simple framework for self-diagnosing your swing and making meaningful improvements. If you're serious about building a more consistent golf swing, hitting cleaner shots, and understanding ball flight laws, this is a must-watch lesson.

videos.title: MG S9 Review: The best 7-seat SUV I’ve ever tested?
videos.summary_source_text:
MG S9 Review: The best 7-seat SUV I’ve ever tested? This is our in-depth review of the all-new 7-seater MG S9. We’ll review the design, the interior, what it’s like to drive, and determine whether you should avoid, consider, shortlist, or buy it.

Explore new & used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-2206

This is the all-new MG S9, and it might just be the best value car you can buy in 2026! 

It’s a plug-in hybrid 7-seater, so Mat’s going to see if this budget-option MG has what it takes to compare with some of the most popular three-row SUVs around, such as the Chery Tiggo 8, Hyundai Ioniq 9, and Peugeot 5008! 

Starting with the design, there’s no denying that it looks anything like a budget option! There’s a large & wide grille up the front that gives the car presence on the road. Along the side, there are elements of chrome trim to help the car stand out, and around the back there’s a wraparound taillight (and unfortunately, a set of incredible fake exhaust tips). The car is rather large, too, being 5 metres in length and 2 metres wide! 

This length means there is ample room for people in the back, whether they’re in the middle or the back row of seats! Up front, there are some soft-touch materials throughout the cabin, although you don’t have to look too far to find plastic elements dotted throughout. The infotainment system is ok, even if it’s a little dark, and the digital dials screen offers key driving information but little else. Thankfully, though, there are physical buttons for the climate control, there’s ample storage throughout, and the range-topper edition as we have here comes with a Bose Sound System and faux leather seats! 

The MG S9 comes equipped with a 1.5-litre plug-in hybrid engine that is good for 300hp. It also has the ability to drive for up to 62 miles on electric power alone. 

The MG S9 is available on Carwow for just £31,210, rising up to £33,450 for the car we have in this video! So is it worth it? Stick with Mat to see for yourself!

videos.title: Panthers Land Brady Tkachuk in Blockbuster Trade with the Ottawa Senators | DFO Rundown
videos.summary_source_text:
Panthers Land Brady Tkachuk in Blockbuster Trade with the Ottawa Senators | DFO Rundown The NHL offseason just delivered its biggest shocker yet.

Jason Gregor and Jeff Marek break down the blockbuster trade that sends Brady Tkachuk from the Ottawa Senators to the Florida Panthers. Why did Ottawa make the move? What comes next for Steve Staios and the Senators? And does adding Brady alongside Matthew Tkachuk instantly make Florida the team to beat in the Eastern Conference?

The guys discuss the assets Ottawa received, whether more moves are coming, and why this trade may only be the first step in a much larger offseason plan for the Senators.

They also examine Seattle's acquisition of Mackie Samoskevich, the Edmonton Oilers signing Jason Dickinson to a long-term extension, and what Mike Babcock's arrival could mean for Edmonton heading into a crucial season.

Later, Alex Tuch's free-agent future takes center stage. Which teams make the most sense for one of the top names on the market? Could Utah, Los Angeles, or another Western Conference contender land the veteran winger?

Plus, Buy or Sell returns with discussions on NHL rebuilds, Elvis Merzlikins' future in Columbus, Gavin McKenna's rookie point projections, and much more.

The show wraps with a look ahead to the Hockey Hall of Fame announcement, including discussions surrounding Carey Price, Patrice Bergeron, Henrik Zetterberg, Curtis Joseph, Rod Brind'Amour, and other notable candidates.

videos.title: What Impact Could the Brady Tkachuk Trade Have on the Colorado Avalanche?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Impact Could the Brady Tkachuk Trade Have on the Colorado Avalanche? Florida Panthers shock the NHL by uniting the Tkachuk brothers, sending Brady Tkachuk to Florida in a blockbuster trade with the Ottawa Senators. Can this high-profile move reshape the Eastern Conference power dynamic—and what does it mean for the Colorado Avalanche’s Stanley Cup aspirations?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the trade’s ripple effects, debating whether Ottawa’s haul of first-round picks signals a smart rebuild or fan frustration. The hosts explore potential targets for the Senators, including a possible pursuit of Valeri Nichushkin or Jason Robertson, and weigh the trade value of key Avalanche assets. Plus, Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson hand out season grades for Cale Makar, analyze Florida’s gritty roster construction, and challenge each other to say something nice about the reigning champions. Will Ottawa flip their assets or keep building? Is Makar’s “A” grade well-earned after a tough postseason? Tune in for spicy NHL insights and trade buzz.

videos.summary_source_text:
China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human) China’s new AI robot MOYA just shocked the internet with warm skin, camera eyes, human-like reactions, and a walking claim that feels way too close to real. But MOYA is only part of a much bigger humanoid robot week, with Boston Dynamics pushing Atlas toward real factory work and Alibaba launching Qwen-Robot as a new AI brain for physical machines.

✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com

MOYA shocks people with warm skin and lifelike reactions
SOURCE: https://blog.robozaps.com/b/droidup-moya-review
Boston Dynamics Atlas moves closer to real factory autonomy
SOURCE: https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10771905
Atlas shows signs of factory intelligence in new reports
SOURCE: https://azertag.az/en/xeber/boston_dynamics_atlas_shows_signs_of_general_intelligence_for_factory_work_reports_say-4269047
Alibaba launches Qwen-Robot for embodied AI machines
SOURCE: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/alibaba-unveils-ai-models-robots-amid-shift-chatbots-agents-2026-06-16/
Alibaba pushes Qwen AI beyond chatbots and into robots
SOURCE: https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3357260/alibaba-eyes-physical-world-its-first-suite-ai-models-robots

This is bigger than one creepy robot demo. Humanoid robots are moving from staged tricks into real interaction, real navigation, real manipulation, and real factory tasks. If this pace continues, robots like MOYA, Atlas, and Qwen-powered machines could start entering hospitals, museums, warehouses, and public spaces much faster than expected.

videos.title: Ottawa Senators TRADE Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers | INSTANT ANALYSIS
videos.summary_source_text:
Ottawa Senators TRADE Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers | INSTANT ANALYSIS David Pagnotta breaks down the biggest trade of the NHL offseason so far.

The Ottawa Senators have traded captain Brady Tkachuk to the Florida Panthers, where he’ll join his brother Matthew Tkachuk in South Florida.

Florida lands a massive franchise-changing forward, while Ottawa receives a huge haul of draft picks, including the 9th overall pick, 25th overall pick, a 2029 first-round pick, and a 2030 second-round pick. The Panthers take on Brady’s full cap hit, while the Senators now face major questions about what comes next after moving on from their captain and one of their best players.

Brady had been connected to trade rumours for months, and once it became clear he may not be part of Ottawa’s long-term future, the Senators made the blockbuster move.

Chaos, shock, and massive implications across the NHL.

Drop your thoughts in the comments — did Ottawa get enough for Brady Tkachuk? Are the Panthers now the team to beat with both Tkachuk brothers together?

videos.title: The Vagus Nerve Hack Doctors Already Use
videos.summary_source_text:
The Vagus Nerve Hack Doctors Already Use The vagus nerve is not just a wellness trend. We have been using vagal stimulation in cardiology for years to help slow certain rapid heart rhythms. Techniques like the Valsalva maneuver work by increasing parasympathetic activity and shifting the body out of stress mode. I think the bigger lesson is that the same system we use in medicine can also help people manage stress, blood pressure, and overall nervous system balance.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

videos.title: Simple Visual Drill for A Better Path #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Simple Visual Drill for A Better Path #golfswing #golftips If you’re struggling with that classic over-the-top move, this simple visual drill can change your club path fast.
Place an impact bag just outside and slightly ahead of the golf ball — it forces the club to work from the inside and follow through toward the target. This instantly helps you stop cutting across and start hitting more solid, straighter shots.
Follow for more clear, practical coaching from Me and My Golf. If you’re struggling with that classic over-the-top move, this simple visual drill can change your club path fast.

videos.title: WHY DO YOU SWEAT SO MUCH BETWEEN YOUR LEGS?   Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
WHY DO YOU SWEAT SO MUCH BETWEEN YOUR LEGS?   Dr. Mandell Do you constantly deal with sweating between your legs, damp underwear, chafing, irritation, itching, odor, or discomfort throughout the day? You're not alone. In this video, I explain why excessive sweating commonly occurs in the groin area, what factors make it worse, and why the sweat itself is often not the real problem. You'll learn how body temperature, friction, clothing choices, weight, exercise, humidity, stress, hormones, and certain medical conditions can contribute to excessive moisture in this sensitive area. More importantly, I'll share practical solutions that can help you stay cooler, drier, and more comfortable, including simple lifestyle changes, hygiene tips, clothing recommendations, skin protection strategies, and when it may be time to speak with your healthcare provider. Understanding what's causing the problem is the first step toward finding lasting relief and improving your daily comfort and confidence.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

videos.title: Shoot The Puck: Kent "Nubben" Norberg – Timrå ska göra sig av med en eller två spelare
videos.summary_source_text:
Shoot The Puck: Kent "Nubben" Norberg – Timrå ska göra sig av med en eller två spelare Shoot The Puck är en podcast av hockeynews.se

Kent ”Nubben” Norberg är en av svensk hockeys största profiler och nu är han på väg in i sin 25:e säsong i Timrå IK. Han har dessutom tre år i Frölunda och två år i Jönköping. ”Nubben” vet vad det innebär att vara sportchef i SHL och nu står han dessutom inför en ombyggnation i klubben.
Livet efter Dahlén och Pettersson med ny huvudtränare och nya viktiga nyckelspelare – men han backas av ingen mindre än Henrik Zetterberg som visar ”oväntat stort” engagemang i klubben.
Nu gästar ”Nubben” Shoot The Puck.

videos.title: OpenAI Just Admitted the Model Was Never the Problem
videos.summary_source_text:
OpenAI Just Admitted the Model Was Never the Problem Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone keeps telling you AI is about to change everything — yet your week looks identical and your subscription keeps creeping up. This week, the people who build these models quietly explained why. We break down three moves and separate the real signal from the launch-day theater: OpenAI's new Partner Network, Adobe's AI assistants landing inside Photoshop and Premiere, and Moonshot's Kimi K2.7-Code.

We read the fine print so you don't have to:
- Why OpenAI's own announcement reads like an admission that the model was never the bottleneck
- The 300,000-consultant number everyone repeated — and why it's a goal, not a fact
- What Adobe's assistants actually automate (and the pricing question Adobe pointedly didn't answer)
- The truth behind "Kimi K2.7-Code beats Claude Opus 4.8" — one benchmark, company-reported, unverified

The demo was always the easy part. Here's what these three announcements really mean for your actual workflow.

📌 Which of these three changes YOUR week? Drop it in the comments — the best ones decide what I stress-test next.

🔔 Subscribe for AI news with the fine print checked, not the press release reprinted.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 Why your AI tools still don't help
00:56 OpenAI's Partner Network — the confession
04:07 Adobe's AI assistants — attacking the busywork
07:07 Moonshot's Kimi K2.7-Code — reading the hype carefully

videos.title: The Two Main Kinds Of Turns In Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
The Two Main Kinds Of Turns In Hockey The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Claude 5's Return: What Anthropic Isn't Saying in Seoul
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude 5's Return: What Anthropic Isn't Saying in Seoul Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic shipped the most capable model it had ever built — and three days later the US government forced it offline for the entire planet, with about 90 minutes of warning. No hype, no vendor spin taken at face value. Here's what "Claude 5" actually is (starting with the fact that it isn't called Claude 5), what it could do, why Commerce pulled it, and whether you'll ever get to use it.

We separate what's verified from what's still a vendor claim: the 1M-token context window, "adaptive thinking," the Stripe migration anecdote, the analytics benchmark numbers, and the export-control directive that took Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dark. Then we get into the real fight underneath it all — not the benchmarks, but governance.

⏱️ Chapters
00:00  Intro: The Fastest Launch Reversal I've Seen
01:08  The Launch and the Shutdown
04:09  What Claude 5 Actually Is (Features & Architecture)
07:13  What It Could Do: Capabilities and Use Cases
09:46  The Risks, and the Part Washington Cared About
12:11  How It Stacks Up Against the Competition
14:04  Roadmap, and What Happens Next

Where's YOUR line between AI safety and regulatory overreach? Drop it in the comments — I read them before planning the next deep dive.

👉 Subscribe for breakdowns that take the policy as seriously as the tech.

videos.title: Why 50% of Your Practice Time Is Probably Wasted | The Train 2.0 Show
videos.summary_source_text:
Why 50% of Your Practice Time Is Probably Wasted | The Train 2.0 Show Why 50% of Your Practice Time Is Probably Wasted | The Train 2.0 Show

If you measure how long NHL players actually spend in their forward stride during a shift, it's around 10% of their ice time. Yet most hockey camps and skill development programs spend 50% of every session drilling stride mechanics in a straight line. That math alone explains a huge chunk of why traditional power skating drills don't translate to a real game.

In this episode of the Train 2.0 Show, Kevin McKinnon sits down with Colton Keller, a Buffalo coach who picked up hockey late in his teens and used Train 2.0 to accelerate his own development before founding Train 2.0 Buffalo. They get into the specific skills the modern NHL is built on that almost nobody is training, the drills players are still wasting ice time on, and what the next generation development model should actually look like.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Fenix-Saaben provkörd!
videos.summary_source_text:
Fenix-Saaben provkörd! Del 5 av serien filmer om Saab-Fenix 9-3 som tyvärr inte kom i serieproduktion.
Det är äntligen dags att provköra chassit och drivlinan som skulle ha funnits i Saab 9-3-generationen som aldrig kom i produktion.
Tyvärr var inte givaren till styrservot inkopplat och hjulinställning var inte heller gjord – så bilen kommer vara bättre att köra än vad den var i denna film. Återkommer med en ny provkörning längre fram!

Anders Johansson som driver Griffin Cars med Gabriel Palmenäs,  berättar också om Saab-tiden och hur nedläggningen av Saab påverkade Trollhättan. 

Bakgrund:
Efterföljaren till den sista generationen Saab 9-3 hade kommit mycket långt, när Saab gick i konkurs. Projekt 540, eller Phoenix/Fenix,  hade bland annat en ny avancerad bak- och framvagn, ny drivlina och även en elbakaxel på gång.
Anders Johansson och Gabriel Palmenäs (som givetvis jobbade på Saab när det begav sig) driver företaget Griffin Cars, och har köpt upp ett restlager av axlar och motorer för att modernisera befintliga Saab 9-3 bilar.
Men den stora planen är att bygga en liten serie moderna bilar, med äkta Saabteknik! (Det återkommer vi till längre fram)
I en serie intervjuer med Saab-folk som var med och utvecklade Fenixplattformen, ska vi tala teknik, men givetvis också provköra en av de uppdaterade bilarna.
I filmerna:
Del 1: bakgrund och framtidsversion
Del 2: Chassit – Gunnar Olsson
och Per Janssson
Del 3: Elbakaxel, Stefan Barbunopulos
Del 4: Motor, Peter Dahl
Del 5: Provkörning!
Del 6: Intervju med designern Jason Castriota

Se till att prenumerera så du inte missar något!

videos.title: Why Stillness Hurts Your Swing  #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Stillness Hurts Your Swing  #golfswing #golftips A stable golf swing does not mean a still golf swing. When golfers try to keep the lower body frozen, they block rotation, lose their pivot, and often come over the top in the downswing. In this video, we show how to keep your feet grounded and stable while allowing the hips to move freely in the backswing. The result is a more athletic, dynamic motion that creates better rotation and cleaner contact.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. A stable golf swing does not mean a still golf swing. videos.title: What's REALLY Keeping Your Blood Pressure High (& How to Fix It)
videos.summary_source_text:
What's REALLY Keeping Your Blood Pressure High (& How to Fix It) Your blood pressure medication may be lowering one of the very nutrients needed to support healthy blood pressure. Discover what causes high blood pressure and why your blood pressure won’t go down, despite treatment.
  
0:00 Why your high blood pressure won’t go down
0:20 Thiazide diuretics and magnesium depletion 
1:48 Calcium channel blockers 
2:16 Magnesium and potassium 
4:12 Vitamin D deficiency and hypertension 
5:27 What causes high blood pressure?
7:56 Sodium and high blood pressure 
8:53 How to lower blood pressure 

This video is for educational purposes only, not medical advice. Do not start, stop, or change medication without your physician.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4xBlO9n

Keep watching to learn more about the key minerals that affect your blood pressure:
▶️ https://youtu.be/Qgwu_-6wy40
▶️ https://youtu.be/2qu59nPJXcg
▶️ https://youtu.be/9dDJY6MLWhk

Check out this article for 7 tips to lower blood pressure naturally: https://drbrg.co/4xGeGs4

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Thiazide diuretics are the most commonly prescribed blood pressure medications and are taken by more than 40 million people. While they help remove excess sodium, they can also deplete magnesium, an essential mineral involved in blood pressure control.

Without enough magnesium, calcium can build up inside the arteries, causing them to become more constricted. This is why calcium channel blockers are commonly used for high blood pressure. Magnesium acts as a natural calcium channel blocker and natural beta blocker. 

Many diuretics for high blood pressure can also deplete potassium. Potassium and magnesium work together to help keep the arteries relaxed, yet deficiencies in both nutrients are very common. Potassium also depends on adequate magnesium to function properly.

Vitamin D deficiency may also contribute to hypertension, and vitamin D relies on magnesium for proper activation and function.

High blood pressure is often viewed as a sodium problem, but focusing solely on reducing sodium intake may overlook other important factors and cause other problems. Addressing potassium and magnesium deficiencies could be a better way of supporting healthy blood pressure levels.

DATA:
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2161831322011279
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0270929506001434 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6695889/

The 131st Amateur Championship came to a dramatic conclusion at Royal Liverpool as two players battled over 36 holes for one of the most prestigious titles in amateur golf.

Watch the best moments from a gripping final that had momentum swings, clutch shots and plenty of drama before a champion was crowned.

The Amateur Championship is one of the leading events in world amateur golf and offers the champion exemptions into The Open, the U.S. Open and, by tradition, an invitation to compete at the Masters Tournament.

videos.title: Is the Most Important Draft of 2020's Ahead for the Denver Nuggets? | Serbian Corner
videos.summary_source_text:
Is the Most Important Draft of 2020's Ahead for the Denver Nuggets? | Serbian Corner As the New York Knicks' fans try to celebrate their first NBA championship in 53 years, the rest of the league has already moved on. The NBA draft is in just 3 days, and it might be the most important one for the Nuggets of the 2020s.
NCAA basketball and NBA draft expert CT Fazio of "Court Talk with CT" joins Miroslav to discuss the moves the Nuggets should make on draft night and in the offseason, before assessing 7 different Draft prospects that might be in the Nuggets' range.
